title: MCP McpTransport trait: request()/close() not object-safe-friendly, HTTP close is a no-op (LSP violation)
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

**Dimension:** SOLID (LSP — Liskov Substitution)

**Location:** /home/doll/OpenClaudia/src/mcp.rs lines 125-131 (trait), 265-273 (StdioTransport::close), 339-342 (HttpTransport::close)

**Violation:**
The McpTransport trait declares 'async fn close(&self) -> Result<(), McpError>'. StdioTransport::close kills the child process. HttpTransport::close (line 340) is a no-op returning Ok(()) with a comment 'HTTP transport doesn't need explicit close'.

Liskov Substitution: callers relying on 'after close(), the server is no longer reachable' hold for Stdio but NOT for HTTP — HTTP keeps the reqwest connection pool alive globally. A session that disconnects an HTTP MCP server still leaks the underlying pool's TCP keepalives and may continue receiving responses to outstanding requests.

Additionally:
1. **No trait method to probe connection health.** The caller cannot tell if a transport is alive without issuing a ping.
2. **request() takes &self but StdioTransport internally locks a Mutex<Child>** (line 195) — this means concurrent request() calls serialize, making the transport effectively single-threaded while pretending otherwise in the trait.
3. **HttpTransport has no retry/timeout of its own.** request() blocks for the reqwest default (which can be many minutes) — no shape matching the 10 MB stdio limit on the HTTP side either.

**Mandated Refactor:**
1. Rename close() to shutdown() and define the contract precisely: 'After shutdown, all outstanding requests MUST return McpError::NotConnected; the transport MUST NOT dial new connections.' Implement in HttpTransport by disabling the client (wrap client in Option<reqwest::Client> and set to None on shutdown).
2. Add a trait method: async fn is_healthy(&self) -> bool. Stdio implements by checking child.try_wait(). Http implements by HEAD-ing a configured healthcheck URL.
3. Document the 'serialised requests' contract on StdioTransport, or use a channel-based actor to pipeline requests (correlation via JSON-RPC id).
